column5: Description
Confirm the pilot cohort, launch platform, workflow, and success measures before implementation cost is committed.

column4: [P0-PROD-01] Confirm pilot cohort and launch platform
column5: Recruit at least 12 representative owner-operators; record device platform and OS, equipment type, factoring use, and availability; document the native launch-platform decision and go/no-go rationale.
Walk at least five recruited drivers through evaluate, accept, prove, invoice, and payment scenarios; record current artifacts and top failure points; reconcile findings with P0 scope.

column4: [P0-PROD-03] Define the pilot scorecard and feedback loop
column5: Assign a source and review cadence to every business validation goal; define privacy-safe events; provide an in-app feedback and pilot support path.
Deliver the complete accessible driver-facing workflow using injected state and services with no direct vendor SDK calls from views.

column4: [P0-MOB-01] Build the app shell and navigation
column5: Provide authenticated and unauthenticated roots; make active-load, modal, and deep-link navigation state restorable and testable; support loading, empty, offline, syncing, failed, and retry states.
Support sign-up, sign-in, password reset, business identity, invoice settings, optional logo, payment terms, and factoring remittance details; validate required invoice fields.

column4: [P0-MOB-03] Build truck and cost profile screens
column5: Capture equipment, MPG, fuel, maintenance reserve, fixed costs, working miles, default fees, and profit target; explain missing inputs and show derived fixed cost per mile.
Show active next actions, delivered-not-invoiced work, receivable states, and monthly summaries; search required fields; distinguish estimated, billed, and paid values.
Support manual load entry and rate-confirmation attachment; keep deadhead visible; recalculate immediately; allow per-load overrides; save evaluating, accepted, or rejected.

column4: [P0-MOB-06] Build active load, stops, status, and ETA
column5: Show stops, appointments, next action, allowed transitions, arrival/departure, corrections, ETA publishing, and manual location fallback; remain usable offline.
Start detention from arrival in no more than two taps; show free, elapsed, billable, and estimated amounts; capture supported charges and evidence; require override reasons.
Scan or import supported images and PDFs; review, edit, classify, and annotate; show queued, uploading, synced, failed, inspect, and retry states by load.
Review line items and evidence, warn about missing documents, generate and share a packet, mark sent, and record full, partial, disputed, overdue, and paid states.
Create, preview, share, shorten expiry, and revoke a per-load link; make sharing optional; show exactly which fields the broker can see.

column4: [P0-MOB-11] Build settings, support, and data-rights UI
column5: Edit defaults; expose app/device-aware feedback; initiate data export and account deletion with confirmation and status; show privacy and support links.

column4: [P0-F03] Mobile Core, Offline, and Device Services
column5: Provide deterministic domain behavior and a durable local-first data layer behind app-owned protocols.

column4: [P0-CORE-01] Model the load domain and trip state machine
column5: Define UUID-based entities and allowed transitions; preserve immutable events and corrections; support cancelled and disputed paths; test invalid transitions.
Implement all FR-3 formulas with decimal-safe arithmetic; expose inputs and missing-input errors; test zero miles, invalid MPG, percentages, rounding, and overrides.

column4: [P0-CORE-03] Implement detention, invoice, and payment calculations
column5: Apply free time; link evidence; version financial revisions; enforce sequential account invoice numbers; reconcile partial payments and remaining balance with tests.

column4: [P0-CORE-04] Implement local models, migrations, and repositories
column5: Persist the active workflow, profile, recent documents, and sync metadata; use explicit migrations; keep UI and domain independent of persistence framework details.

column4: [P0-CORE-05] Implement the durable outbox and synchronization engine
column5: Commit offline mutations with idempotency keys; retry after restart and reconnection; expose sync state; prevent duplicates; require review for financial conflicts.
Store session material securely; restore authenticated offline use; handle expiry and refresh without losing local work; prevent cross-account local data leakage.
Capture UTC, timezone, latitude, longitude, and accuracy on explicit active-load actions; label denied, unavailable, inaccurate, and manual events truthfully.
Retain private originals until confirmed upload; validate and process files, hash bytes, queue background transfer, retry safely, and exclude document data from telemetry.

column4: [P0-CORE-09] Implement versioned invoice PDF generation and sharing
column5: Generate reproducible US Letter invoices; include selected evidence; create revisions rather than replacing history; hand files to the platform share sheet.

column4: [P0-CORE-10] Implement ETA estimation and navigation handoff
column5: Store manual or on-device estimates with source and freshness; hand navigation to native maps; never present estimates as truck-safe routing.
Provide authenticated data, private files, tested row-level authorization, idempotent sync, and narrowly scoped public tracking reads.

column4: [P0-BE-01] Establish backend environments and migration workflow
column5: Create separate local/development and pilot configuration; version schema, policies, functions, and seeds; document reset/deploy; keep service credentials out of clients and source control.
Support email/password registration, reset, session refresh, and profile ownership; store invoice and factoring details; enforce required server values.
Create constrained tables for the P0 domain; use client UUIDs and UTC timestamps; preserve immutable evidence and invoice revisions.
Enable RLS on every owned table; scope rows to authenticated users; prove cross-account reads and writes fail; restrict service-role use.
Use private buckets and account/load/document object keys; validate ownership and metadata; issue short-lived signed URLs; prevent permanent or enumerable access.
Accept client UUIDs and idempotency keys; return stable retry results; expose reconciliation metadata; reject invalid state and financial writes atomically.
Use tokens with at least 128 bits of randomness and persist only hashes; scope visibility; support preview, expiry, shortened expiry, and immediate revocation.

column4: [P0-BE-08] Implement the public tracking read contract
column5: Validate share tokens in an Edge Function; return only approved status fields; never expose coordinates, financials, private documents, or authenticated APIs.
Export user-controlled data; revoke shares and sessions; delete or schedule deletion of owned rows and files; report progress and recoverable failures.
Give a broker a secure, account-free, mobile-friendly status page for exactly one shared load.
Render only approved carrier, load, scoped stop, status, ETA, event, delay, freshness, and POD-availability fields from the public contract.
Show the last successful update and estimate source; distinguish loading, current, stale, delayed, delivered, and no-update states; never imply live GPS.
Handle invalid, expired, and revoked links safely; prevent cross-load cache leaks; pass keyboard, screen-reader, text scaling, and WCAG AA checks.

column4: [P0-WEB-04] Deploy and harden the tracking site
column5: Deploy the static site; configure TLS, security headers, safe caching, environment separation, monitoring, and a reproducible release command.
Make the pilot observable, supportable, legally shippable, and resilient enough for real loads without database intervention.

column4: [P0-REL-01] Establish source control, builds, and CI gates
column5: Create app and web projects after platform selection; configure build and test jobs and protected secrets; make clean checkouts reproducibly build pilot artifacts.

column4: [P0-REL-02] Implement privacy-safe analytics, diagnostics, and feedback
column5: Instrument scorecard and non-sensitive failures; exclude private payloads and tokens; include app/version/device context in user-initiated feedback.

column4: [P0-REL-03] Automate domain, integration, and authorization tests
column5: Cover calculations, transitions, migrations, sync, document policies, share scope, expiry/revocation, and cross-account denial in CI.
Test offline mutation, termination, reconnection, conflict review, interrupted upload, retry, and duplicate-event scenarios without data loss.
Verify critical screens on two representative devices; meet accessibility expectations; reach cached active load within three seconds on the chosen device.

column4: [P0-REL-06] Complete privacy, legal, and store readiness
column5: Publish privacy, terms, support, deletion instructions, and accurate store disclosures; state product limitations; verify contextual permission prompts.

column4: [P0-REL-07] Prepare and distribute the closed pilot
column5: Configure signing, pilot environments, cost limits, monitoring, beta distribution, tester onboarding, support ownership, incident contact, and feedback cadence.

column4: [P0-REL-08] Complete a real load from evaluation to payment
column5: Use a production-like pilot build for one real end-to-end load without direct database intervention; block launch on unresolved data-loss, authorization, or financial errors.
